brief: Design a 90-day plan to reduce prior authorization delays without increasing denial risk or staff burnout.

deliverable.audience: hospital COO and revenue cycle leadership

facts: Clinic processes 4,800 prior authorizations per month.
Median time to submission is 3.2 days; payer response is 5.8 days.
Seven payers account for 74 percent of delayed authorizations.
Nursing staff perform 38 percent of documentation follow-up even when no clinical judgment is required.
EHR fields are structured but supporting documents are inconsistent.
Automation can draft payer checklists but cannot submit or sign attestations.
Compliance is concerned about hallucinated clinical facts and unauthorized practice of medicine.
Patients report cancelled appointments when auth is not complete 48 hours before care.
conflicting_signals: Automation reduces clerical burden but creates compliance risk.
Payer-specific workflows improve success but add complexity.

grounding_rule: Use only this context pack. Do not invent facts. Label uncertainty when context is incomplete.
